question.label: Useful introductions
question.name: Who would be most useful for you to spend live time with next?
question.description: Helps line up the next relationship that will improve his map of how the team actually works.

answer: If I know exactly what ownership I should take this month, I can contribute with more confidence.

assessment.note: He pivoted from introductions to needing clear ownership this month, signaling mild uncertainty that is dampening confidence; [THREAD-DEFERRED-WINDDOWN] ownership this month.

new_queue.label: Steady next steps
new_queue.name: What should we line up before our next check-in?

new_queue.label: Steady next steps
new_queue.name: What support from me would make the biggest difference before our next check-in?

history.answer_excerpt: A short weekly architecture review with someone senior would help me calibrate faster.
If I know exactly what ownership I should take this month, I can contribute with more confidence.
I would benefit from a clearer guide for what to escalate quickly versus what to batch.
The runbook helped, but some practical handoff details only show up during live incidents.

I am comfortable reading the services, but I am less sure how decisions are really made here.
I can trace most request paths now, though I still miss historical context behind certain tradeoffs.
I have not been fully clear on who is on point for each domain when priorities conflict.
I am trying not to interrupt people, so sometimes I keep questions in a notes doc instead of asking.
